summary: Recent changes to the session negotiation process within Firefox focus on improving memory management related to handling Session Description Protocol (SDP) attributes, which are essential for establishing multimedia sessions like video calls. By using **UniquePtrs**, a smart pointer that automatically manages memory, Firefox enhances safety and efficiency, reducing the risk of memory leaks associated with traditional pointer usage. These modifications mainly involve how SDP attributes are created, stored, and copied within the underlying code, fostering a cleaner and more robust implementation while _not changing any visible user features_.
